item.title: BrahMos: The World’s Fastest Anti-Ship Cruise Missile Explained
item.description: When it comes to modern missile technology, few names command as much global attention as BrahMos—a missile system renowned for its unmatched speed, precision, and versatility. Recognized as the world’s fastest operational anti-ship cruise missile, BrahMos symbolizes India’s rapidly advancing defense capabilities and its strategic partnership
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Water for Pakistan: Lifeline Dependency & Geopolitical Control
item.description: Water is not just a natural resource for Pakistan—it is its lifeline. With over 90% of its water supply coming from a single river system, Pakistan is critically dependent on external sources. Agriculture, power generation, and daily life across the country rely on uninterrupted water flow, primarily from rivers originating in Indian-administered territory. In recent developments, however
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: RCB, Alcohol & Public Spectacles: Culture, Ownership & Controversy
item.description: Every April–May from past 18 years, Bengaluru transforms into a sea of red and black as fans don RCB jerseys and pour into pubs, chanting their team’s praises. For many, RCB (Royal Challengers Bengaluru) isn’t just a cricket franchise—it’s
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Why Madanapalle Should Be Annamayya District HQ: Key Reasons
item.description: Madanapalle has always been a place of cultural depth, historical value, and administrative importance. Over the years, many discussions have taken place regarding the formation of the proposed
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Abhaya Kshethram: A Sanctuary of Hope & Dignity
item.description: Nestled near Renigunta Road in Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh, Abhaya Kshethram is a charitable home dedicated to providing care, dignity, and hope to those society often overlooks: physically and mentally challenged orphans, destitute women, and the elderly. (Abhaya Kshethram) Founding and Vision Founded i
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill 2025 – What does it propose?
item.description: New Delhi— The Union government has introduced the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirtieth Amendment) Bill, 2025, a measure that would force the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers, and all Union/State (and NCT of Delhi) ministers to demit office if they remain in custody for 30 consecutive days in
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Karnataka Editors’ Jail Sentence Sparks Allegations of Intimidation
item.description: Bengaluru The controversy surrounding the Karnataka Assembly’s decision to sentence two editors to one year in prison for alleged defamation has deepened. Reports reveal one editor claims ongoing intimidation from a sitting legislator. Assembly Verdict and Legal Roadmap On June 21, 2017, the Assembly’s Privileges Committee found Anil Raj
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: India Stands Tall: Navigating Geopolitical Tensions Without Bowing to Pressure
item.description: In today’s complex and volatile global environment, India is emerging not just as a regional power, but as a nation capable of making sovereign choices amidst growing international scrutiny. With Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the helm, India has
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: The MiG-21: India’s First Supersonic Jet Retires After 62 Years of Glorious Service
item.description: India’s journey toward becoming a modern air power took flight with the MiG‑21, a supersonic fighter aircraft that served the nation with distinction for over six decades. As the Indian Air Force prepares to bid farewell to
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Donald Trump and the Elusive Nobel Peace Prize: Past Attempts, Present Claims, and Persistent Barriers
item.description: Few honors in the world carry as much symbolic and moral weight as the Nobel Peace Prize. It’s not just a medal—it’s a historic recognition of an individual's contribution to peace and human progress. Donald Trump, former U.S.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Maulana Masood Azhar, The Terror Mastermind and Most Wanted by India
item.description: Maulana Masood Azhar is one of the world’s most dangerous and most wanted terrorists, known for orchestrating multiple deadly attacks against India. As the founder of Jaish-e-Mohammed (JeM), a UN-designated terrorist organization, Azhar has spent decades fueling jihadist violence, especially in the Indian region of Jammu and Kashmir. His name is synonymous with some of the worst terror incide
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: 'PigeonBot' is the first robot that can bend its wings like a real bird
item.description: Pigeons may be considered rats of the sky, but some scientists have found greater value in these urban birds: the blueprint for a new generation of flying machines. Birds can modify the shape of their wings by fanning out their feathers or shuffling them closer together. Those adjustments allow birds to cut through the sky more nimbly than rigid drones. Now, using new i
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Privatisation of Public Sector Banks & Banks Strike in March 2021
item.description: The Finance Minister has announced in Union Budget of India for 2021-22, that two Public Sector Banks (PSBs) will be Privatised. The policy of government in last three decades of economic reforms was to sell a part of shares in Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) including PSBs to public while retaining the controlling share in its own hands.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: International Mother Language Day
item.description: International Mother Language Day is a worldwide annual observance held on 21st February to promote awareness of linguistic and cultural diversity and to promote multilingualism.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: If farmers have greater choice to sell to whoever & wherever! Why still Farmers are protesting?
item.description: The Rajya Sabha passed two out of the three Bills being referred to as the Farm Bills. These two Bills are the Farmers Produce Trade and Commerce (Promotion and Facilitation) Bill, 2020 and the Farmers (Empowerment and Protection) Agreement on Price Assurance and Farm Services Bill, 2020. The Lok Sabha had already passed these Bills. There was
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: World Cancer Day, February 4th - Early detection of Cancer can lead to Improved outcomes & Cure.
item.description: Cancer is a large group of diseases that can start in almost any organ or tissue of the body when abnormal cells grow uncontrollably, go beyond their usual boundaries to invade adjoining parts of the body and/or spread to other organs. The latter process is called metastasizing which is a major cause of death from cancer. A neoplasm and malignant tumour are oth
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Indian Farms Reforms of 2020
item.description: The Indian Farms Reforms of 2020 refer to the agricultural bills passed by the Lok Sabha on 17th September 2020 and by the Rajya Sabha on 20th September 2020. The bills collectively seek to provide farmers with multiple marketing channels and provide a legal framework for farmers to enter into pre-arranged contracts among other things.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: For Indian Youth..! What should you know about Nambi Narayanan?
item.description: Lets see the background, In 1992, India signed an agreement with Russia.. Aggrement was for transfer of technology to develop cryogenic fuel-based engines and procurement of two such engines for ₹ 235 crore.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Things to Know before you go for Registration of a Plot or Property in Bangalore
item.description: When you decide to buy a property or land than a normal business deal where you get a plot of land in exchange of money. Before going into details of plot registration, there are a few terms you must know. Registration fee - It's a small percentage of total cost of plot paid by buyer to state government for registering property on his name.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: When Police doesnot register your FIR? Contact Judge!
item.description: If you are reporting a cognisable crime and Police refuse to register your FIR, you can make a complaint to a higher ranking officer such as the Superintendent of Police (SP), t
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: World Wildlife Day
item.description: On 20 December 2013, United Nations General Assembly (UNGA), in its resolution UN 68/205, decided to proclaim 3 March, the international day of the adoption of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) on the planet raise awareness and benefits fauna and flora in 1973, a
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: National Education Policy 2020, Government of India (NEP 2020)
item.description: National Education Policy (NEP 2020) has been approved by Union Cabinet on 29 July 2020. Its major aim is to introduce changes in the Indian Education System right from the school to the college level of education.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Scientists Have Created Hexagonal Salt, For First Time Ever
item.description: While it probably won't make it to your dining table, a new scientific achievement might be able to help in everything from radar equipment to electric cars: scientists have been able to form salt, aka sodium chloride (NaCl), in a hexagonal shapeT
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Droupadi Murmu, New President of India
item.description: Droupadi Murmu was nominated to President elections and on 21st of July 2022 elections counting was finished and Droupadi Murmu won the elections and will be the New President of India, Murmu is likely to take oath on July 25 as President of India. A few highlights of 15th President of India Droupadi Murmu will be the first person belonging to a Scheduled
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: President of India nominated Veerendra Heggade, P.T. Usha, Ilaiyaraaja and V. Vijayendra Prasad to the Rajya Sabha
item.description: According to the Fourth Schedule (Articles 4(1) and 80(2)) of the Constitution of India, The President of India can nominate 12 members to Rajya Sabha for a term of six-years for their contributions towards Arts, Literature, Sciences and Social Services.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Should people really vote in democracy?
item.description: Yes, voting is a crucial aspect of a democratic society and it plays a fundamental role in shaping the governance and policies of a nation. Here are some reasons why voting is important: Representation: Voting allows individuals to have a say in choosing representatives who will make decisions on their behalf. By participating in elections, citizens can he
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K
